classDescription: A full caster built around Bardic Inspiration — short-rest dice handed to allies for attack, save, and skill boosts — combined with access to any skill, Expertise doubling, and a broad support-control spell list.
roleIdentity: Support-controller hybrid with broad utility and magical flexibility.

bard-spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
bard-spellcasting.summary: You gain access to spell slots, a spellcasting ability, and a growing library of magical options.
bard-spellcasting.description:
You use Charisma as your spellcasting ability. Your spell save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Charisma modifier. Your spell attack bonus = proficiency bonus + Charisma modifier.

As a Bard, you know a specific number of spells (not all Bard spells) and can cast them using the Bard spell slot table. You can replace one known spell when you gain a Bard level.

You can also cast any spell you know as a ritual if it has the ritual tag, without expending a spell slot.
bard-spellcasting.effect: Cha-based full caster; know spells (not prepare them); can ritual-cast spells with the ritual tag.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.name: Bardic Inspiration (d6)
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.summary: Grant an ally a die they can spend later to swing a key roll in your party’s favor.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.description:
As a Bonus Action, choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet that you can see. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (a d6 at this level).

Once in the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails.

A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.recovery: Long Rest (Short Rest after gaining Font of Inspiration at level 5).
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.effect: Give an ally a d6 they add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within the next 10 minutes.

bard-jack-of-all-trades.name: Jack of All Trades
bard-jack-of-all-trades.summary: You are broadly competent even outside your best skills.
bard-jack-of-all-trades.description:
You can add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

This applies to a wide range of checks including initiative rolls, and to ability checks made with skills and tools you don't have proficiency in.
bard-jack-of-all-trades.effect: Add half proficiency bonus to all non-proficient ability checks, including initiative.

bard-song-of-rest-d6.name: Song of Rest (d6)
bard-song-of-rest-d6.summary: Your party gets better value from downtime healing during rests.
bard-song-of-rest-d6.description:
At the end of a Short Rest, if you perform music or tell stories to your companions, each friendly creature who spent Hit Dice to regain hit points regains an extra 1d6 hit points.

This bonus increases as you gain Bard levels: 1d6 at levels 2-8, 1d8 at levels 9-12, 1d10 at levels 13-16, and 1d12 at levels 17 and above.

bard-bard-college-subclass.name: Bard College (Subclass)
bard-bard-college-subclass.summary: Choose your college and unlock a stronger identity for the rest of the build.
bard-bard-college-subclass.description:
At 3rd level you choose a Bard College that defines your performance style and expands your magical repertoire. Each college grants unique features at levels 3, 6, and 14.

Options include the College of Dance (acrobatic spellcasting), College of Glamour (fey-touched enchantment), College of Lore (arcane secrets and cutting words), College of Valor (martial combat magic), and others.

bard-expertise.name: Expertise
bard-expertise.summary: You push chosen skills into true specialty territory.
bard-expertise.description:
Choose two skill proficiencies or one skill proficiency and your proficiency with thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

At 10th level, you can choose two more of your skill proficiencies for this benefit.

bard-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

bard-cantrips-known-3.name: Cantrips Known: 3
bard-cantrips-known-3.summary: Your known cantrip count is now 3.
bard-cantrips-known-3.description: Your total known Bard cantrips increases to 3 at this level. Cantrips are at-will magical abilities — they don't consume spell slots. Choose the new cantrip from the Bard cantrip list.

bard-font-of-inspiration.name: Font of Inspiration
bard-font-of-inspiration.summary: Your inspiration engine is now much easier to keep online.
bard-font-of-inspiration.description:
You can now regain all your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short Rest, in addition to a Long Rest.

Before this feature, Bardic Inspiration only recharged on Long Rests. Font of Inspiration transforms it from a limited daily resource into a reliable per-encounter tool.
bard-font-of-inspiration.effect: Bardic Inspiration now recharges on Short Rest or Long Rest.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.summary: Grant an ally a die they can spend later to swing a key roll in your party’s favor.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.description:
As a Bonus Action, choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet that you can see. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (a d8 at this level).

Once in the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the die, but must decide before the DM reveals success or failure.

You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and regain all uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.effect: Give an ally a d8 they add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within the next 10 minutes.

bard-subclass-feature.name: Subclass Feature
bard-subclass-feature.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
bard-subclass-feature.description: Your chosen Bard College grants you an additional feature at this level. The exact ability depends on your college — check your subclass for details.

bard-countercharm.name: Countercharm
bard-countercharm.summary: Use performance or force of presence to protect allies against mental disruption.
bard-countercharm.description:
As an action, you start a countercharm performance — a song, recitation, or motion — that lasts until the start of your next turn. During this time, each friendly creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you has Advantage on saving throws against being Charmed or Frightened.

You can extend the performance each round by using your Bonus Action on subsequent turns.
bard-countercharm.duration: Until start of your next turn (extendable with Bonus Action)
bard-countercharm.effect: Friendly creatures within 30 ft have Advantage on saves against Charmed and Frightened.

bard-magical-secrets.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement-2.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

bard-song-of-rest-d8.name: Song of Rest (d8)
bard-song-of-rest-d8.summary: Your party gets better value from downtime healing during rests.
bard-song-of-rest-d8.description: Your Song of Rest die upgrades to a d8 at this level. When friendly creatures spend Hit Dice during a Short Rest while you perform, each creature who uses at least one Hit Die regains an extra 1d8 hit points.

bard-expertise-2.name: Expertise
bard-expertise-2.summary: You push chosen skills into true specialty territory.
bard-expertise-2.description:
Choose two more skill proficiencies you already have. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check that uses either of those chosen proficiencies.

You now have four skills total benefiting from Expertise (two chosen at level 3, two more at level 10).

bard-magical-secrets-2.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets-2.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets-2.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets-2.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d10.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
bard-bardic-inspiration-d10.summary: Grant an ally a die they can spend later to swing a key roll in your party’s favor.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d10.description:
As a Bonus Action, choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (now a d10 at levels 10-14).

They can add it to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within 10 minutes — after seeing the d20 roll but before the DM reveals success or failure.

You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, and regain all uses on a Short or Long Rest.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d10.effect: Give an ally a d10 they add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within the next 10 minutes.

bard-cantrips-known-4.name: Cantrips Known: 4
bard-cantrips-known-4.summary: Your known cantrip count is now 4.
bard-cantrips-known-4.description: Your total known Bard cantrips increases to 4 at this level. Choose the new cantrip from the Bard cantrip list.

bard-magical-secrets-3.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets-3.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets-3.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets-3.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-ability-score-improvement-3.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement-3.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

bard-song-of-rest-d10.name: Song of Rest (d10)
bard-song-of-rest-d10.summary: Your party gets better value from downtime healing during rests.
bard-song-of-rest-d10.description: Your Song of Rest die upgrades to a d10 at this level. When friendly creatures spend Hit Dice during a Short Rest while you perform, each creature who uses at least one Hit Die regains an extra 1d10 hit points.

bard-magical-secrets-4.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets-4.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets-4.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets-4.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-subclass-feature-2.name: Subclass Feature
bard-subclass-feature-2.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
bard-subclass-feature-2.description: Your Bard College grants you a final capstone feature at this level. The exact ability depends on your college — check your subclass for details.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d12.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
bard-bardic-inspiration-d12.summary: Grant an ally a die they can spend later to swing a key roll in your party’s favor.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d12.description:
As a Bonus Action, choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (now a d12 at levels 15-20).

They can add it to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within 10 minutes — after seeing the d20 roll but before the DM reveals success or failure.

You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, and regain all uses on a Short or Long Rest.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d12.effect: Give an ally a d12 they add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within the next 10 minutes.

bard-ability-score-improvement-4.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement-4.description:
You can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. No score can exceed 20 using this feature.

Alternatively, you can forgo the ability score increases to gain a feat of your choice for which you qualify.

bard-song-of-rest-d12.name: Song of Rest (d12)
bard-song-of-rest-d12.summary: Your party gets better value from downtime healing during rests.
bard-song-of-rest-d12.description: Your Song of Rest die upgrades to a d12 at this level — its maximum. When friendly creatures spend Hit Dice during a Short Rest while you perform, each creature who uses at least one Hit Die regains an extra 1d12 hit points.

bard-magical-secrets-5.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets-5.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets-5.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets-5.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-magical-secrets-6.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets-6.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets-6.description:
You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class’s spell list, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in your spells known. You gain additional Magical Secrets at later Bard levels.
bard-magical-secrets-6.effect: Learn 2 spells from any class spell list; they count as Bard spells.

bard-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
bard-epic-boon.summary: Gain an endgame reward that pushes the build into truly legendary territory.
bard-epic-boon.description:
At 19th level you gain an Epic Boon feat of your choice, or another feat you qualify for. Epic Boon feats are high-power rewards reserved for this tier of play.

Examples include Epic Boon of Spell Recall (cast a spell of level 1-4 without a slot once per Short Rest), Epic Boon of Truesight (gain Truesight out to 60 ft), and Epic Boon of Recovery (when reduced to 0 HP, use reaction to drop to 1 HP instead — 1/LR).

bard-superior-inspiration.name: Superior Inspiration
bard-superior-inspiration.summary: Even when running dry, you are less likely to start an important scene empty-handed.
bard-superior-inspiration.description:
When you roll Initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration remaining, you regain 1 use.

This ensures you always have at least one Bardic Inspiration die available at the start of a new encounter.
bard-superior-inspiration.effect: Automatically regain 1 Bardic Inspiration use when rolling Initiative if you have none remaining.

progressionSummary.summary: Jack of All Trades, Song of Rest (d6).
Expertise, Magical Secrets, Bardic Inspiration (d10), Cantrips Known: 4.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spells and social skills
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves your armor class, initiative, and finesse/ranged attacks
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: low HP and weak Concentration saves can make a Bard feel fragile
